name: Chunk 1/42
text:
# FirstUK Bank — SmartSaver Account Terms & Conditions  
## Metadata  
| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Document ID | SAV-001-TC |
| Version | 2.2 |
| Effective Date | 2026-01-15 |
| Review Date | 2027-01-15 |
| Owner | General Counsel |
| Department | Legal |
| Status | Active |
| Classification | Legal - Customer Facing |
| Audience | Customers, Staff, Regulators |
| Related Documents | [[products]], [[product_pricing_guide_2026]], [[policy_privacy]] |  
---

name: Chunk 2/42
text:
## IMPORTANT INFORMATION  
**Read these terms carefully before opening a SmartSaver Account.**  
These Terms & Conditions form a binding contract between you and FirstUK Bank PLC.  
---

name: Chunk 3/42
text:
## 1. DEFINITIONS & KEY TERMS  
- **"Account"** = Your SmartSaver savings account held with FirstUK Bank
- **"We/Our/Us"** = FirstUK Bank PLC and its employees
- **"You/Your"** = The customer opening the account (account holder)
- **"Business Day"** = Monday–Friday (excluding UK bank holidays)
- **"Deposit"** = Money you place into the account
- **"Withdrawal"** = Money you remove from the account
- **"Interest"** = Money we pay you based on your balance (variable rate)  
---

name: Chunk 4/42
text:
## 2. ACCOUNT OPENING  
### 2.1 Eligibility  
You must be:
- At least 18 years old
- A UK resident
- Able to provide proof of identity (UK Passport or Driving License)
- Able to provide proof of address

name: Chunk 5/42
text:
### 2.2 Application Process  
1. Complete application form (online, branch, or postal)
2. Provide identity verification (KYC)
3. Pass AML screening
4. Receive confirmation (email or letter)
5. Account activated within 1–2 business days

name: Chunk 6/42
text:
### 2.3 Joint Accounts  
Two customers can hold one SmartSaver Account jointly. Both must:
- Complete separate identity verification
- Agree to joint liability (both responsible for account)
- Pass AML screening separately
- Have equal withdrawal rights (either can withdraw full balance)  
---

name: Chunk 7/42
text:
## 3. DEPOSITS & WITHDRAWALS  
### 3.1 Making Deposits  
**Deposit Methods:**
- Bank transfer (FPS, BACS)
- Cash deposit (at branch)
- Check deposit (future feature)  
**Minimum Opening Balance:** £1.00
**Maximum Balance:** Unlimited
**No Waiting Periods:** Deposits available immediately

name: Chunk 8/42
text:
### 3.2 Withdrawals  
**Withdrawal Methods:**
- ATM withdrawal (cash, max £500/day)
- Bank transfer (to another account, no limit)
- Teller withdrawal (branch, no limit)  
**Restrictions:** None. Easy access at any time.  
**Processing:**
- ATM withdrawals: Immediate
- Transfers out: 1–2 business days (FPS immediate, BACS next day)
- Teller: Immediate (if cash available at branch)

name: Chunk 9/42
text:
### 3.3 Dormancy  
If your account has:
- No activity (deposits or withdrawals) for 12 months, AND
- No customer contact for 12 months  
Your account becomes "dormant." We will:
1. Send letter to last known address
2. Mark account as dormant (no impact on you)
3. Continue accruing interest
4. Maintain full access (deposit/withdrawal possible)  
---

name: Chunk 10/42
text:
## 4. INTEREST  
### 4.1 Interest Rates  
Your account earns interest at a **variable rate** based on your balance tier:  
| Tier | Balance | Rate |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | £0–£10,000 | 4.50% AER |
| 2 | £10,001–£50,000 | 4.70% AER |
| 3 | £50,001+ | 4.80% AER |  
**AER** = Annual Equivalent Rate (shows the yearly interest)

name: Chunk 11/42
text:
### 4.2 Interest Calculation  
- **Accrual:** Calculated daily, paid monthly
- **Payment Date:** Last business day of each month
- **Tiering:** Tiered rates apply to balance as of payment date  
**Example (30-day month):**
- Opening balance: £10,000 (Tier 1: 4.50%)
- Interest accrual: £10,000 × 4.50% ÷ 365 days × 30 days = £36.99
- Paid on: Last business day of month

name: Chunk 12/42
text:
### 4.3 Rate Changes  
- **Review Frequency:** Monthly
- **Basis:** Bank of England Base Rate + 2.5% margin (margin fixed)
- **Notice:** Email notification when rate changes
- **Effective:** 1st of following month
- **Your Choice:** Switch to different product if unhappy with rate  
---

name: Chunk 13/42
text:
## 5. FEES & CHARGES  
### 5.1 No Monthly Maintenance Fee  
Your SmartSaver Account has **£0 monthly fee**.

name: Chunk 14/42
text:
### 5.2 Transaction Fees  
| Transaction | Fee |
|---|---|
| Domestic Transfer | £0 |
| ATM Withdrawal (UK) | £0 |
| ATM Withdrawal (International) | £2.50 |
| International Transfer | £5.00 |
| Standing Order Setup | £0 |
| Direct Debit Setup | £0 |

name: Chunk 15/42
text:
### 5.3 Card Fees  
- **Initial Debit Card:** £0
- **Replacement Debit Card:** £15 (or £25 for expedited delivery)  
---

name: Chunk 16/42
text:
## 6. STANDING ORDERS & DIRECT DEBITS  
### 6.1 Standing Orders  
A standing order is a regular, fixed transfer you set up.  
**Setup:**
- Specify payee, amount, frequency (weekly/monthly/etc.)
- Provide receiving account details
- Standing order executes on scheduled date  
**Cancellation:**
- Cancel at any time (no notice period required)
- Cancellation effective immediately
- Future payments blocked

name: Chunk 17/42
text:
### 6.2 Direct Debits  
A direct debit is an authorization for a biller to withdraw money.  
**Setup:**
- Provide authorization (mandate) to biller
- Specify maximum amount
- Biller initiates withdrawals  
**Protection (Direct Debit Guarantee):**
- Refund within 10 business days if unauthorized
- No questions asked
- Full protection under Direct Debit Guarantee  
**Cancellation:**
- Request from bank (5 days notice usually)
- Or contact biller directly  
---

name: Chunk 18/42
text:
## 7. JOINT ACCOUNT PROVISIONS  
### 7.1 Joint & Several Liability  
For joint accounts:
- **Both Signatories Liable:** Either account holder can withdraw full balance
- **Interest Split:** Shared equally (unless you specify otherwise)
- **Disputes:** Both must agree to account closure

name: Chunk 19/42
text:
### 7.2 Death of Account Holder  
If one joint account holder dies:
- Surviving holder retains full account access
- Estate of deceased entitled to their share (50%)
- Bank requires death certificate and documentation

name: Chunk 20/42
text:
### 7.3 Divorce/Separation  
If joint account holders separate:
- Both retain legal rights to full account balance
- Either can freeze/close account (may require court order)
- Bank cannot determine who "owns" what (civil matter)
- Recommend legal advice  
---

name: Chunk 21/42
text:
## 8. PRIVACY & DATA PROTECTION  
### 8.1 Personal Data  
We collect personal data to:
- Open and manage your account
- Comply with legal/regulatory requirements (KYC, AML)
- Prevent fraud
- Market products (if you consent)

name: Chunk 22/42
text:
### 8.2 Your Rights (GDPR)  
You have the right to:
- **Access:** Request copy of your personal data
- **Correction:** Correct inaccurate data
- **Erasure:** Request deletion (with exceptions for legal holds)
- **Portability:** Get your data in portable format  
**Contact:** dpo@firstuk.bank (Data Protection Officer)

name: Chunk 23/42
text:
### 8.3 Third-Party Data Sharing  
We share data with:
- Credit reference agencies (for credit assessment, if applicable)
- Regulatory authorities (FCA, PRA, if required)
- Law enforcement (if court-ordered)  
**We do NOT sell your data to marketers.**  
---

name: Chunk 24/42
text:
## 9. ACCOUNT CLOSURE  
### 9.1 Customer-Initiated Closure  
You can close your account at any time by:
- Visiting a branch
- Calling 0800-555-2265
- Submitting online form

name: Chunk 25/42
text:
### 9.2 Closure Process  
1. **Request Received:** We verify your identity
2. **Final Balance:** Calculate balance including any pending interest
3. **Standing Orders/DDs:** Cancelled (effective immediately)
4. **Final Payment:** Transfer balance via bank transfer, check, or cash
5. **Confirmation:** Letter sent confirming closure
6. **Records Archived:** Documents kept for 6 years (regulatory requirement)

name: Chunk 26/42
text:
### 9.3 Bank-Initiated Closure  
We may close your account if:
- You breach these terms & conditions
- We suspect fraud or unauthorized use
- Regulatory order requires closure
- You breach AML requirements  
**Notice:** We give 30 days notice (except emergencies)  
---

name: Chunk 27/42
text:
## 10. COMPLAINTS & DISPUTE RESOLUTION  
### 10.1 Complaint Procedure  
If you're unhappy with our service:  
1. **Contact Us:** Phone 0800-555-2265, email support@firstuk.bank, or write
2. **We Respond:** Within 8 calendar days (or up to 8 weeks if complex)
3. **Resolution:** We'll explain decision and next steps

name: Chunk 28/42
text:
### 10.2 Unresolved Complaints  
If unhappy with our resolution, you can escalate to:  
**Financial Ombudsman Service (FOS)**
- Phone: 0800 023 4567
- Website: www.financialombudsman.org.uk
- Free and independent  
FOS can award compensation up to £385,000 (if we at fault).  
---

name: Chunk 29/42
text:
## 11. BANK LIABILITY & LIMITATIONS  
### 11.1 What We're Liable For  
We're responsible for:
- ✅ Failing to process transactions correctly
- ✅ Unauthorized transactions (fraud)
- ✅ Breach of confidentiality
- ✅ System failures/outages

name: Chunk 30/42
text:
### 11.2 What We're NOT Liable For  
We're not responsible for:
- ❌ Delays in external systems (payment schemes)
- ❌ Your loss if you disclose your PIN/password
- ❌ Fraud if you were negligent (left card visible)
- ❌ Interest rate changes (variable product)
- ❌ Third-party actions (you authorize)  
---

name: Chunk 31/42
text:
## 12. FRAUD & UNAUTHORIZED TRANSACTIONS  
### 12.1 Your Responsibility  
You must:
- Protect your PIN and password
- Report fraud/suspicious activity immediately
- Use ATMs in safe locations
- Don't share security details

name: Chunk 32/42
text:
### 12.2 Fraud Reimbursement  
**If fraud occurs:**
- Report immediately: 0800-555-FRAUD
- We investigate (typically 10 business days)
- **If unauthorized:** Full refund within 10 days (no customer liability)
- **If customer negligent:** We may share liability (case-by-case)  
---

name: Chunk 33/42
text:
## 13. CHANGES TO THESE TERMS  
### 13.1 Our Right to Change Terms  
We can change these terms if:
- We give 30 days written notice
- Change is in our or your legitimate interest
- Change reflects regulatory changes

name: Chunk 34/42
text:
### 13.2 Your Right if Terms Change  
If you disagree with changes:
- You have 60 days to close your account
- No penalties or early closure fees
- Final balance transferred without charge  
---

name: Chunk 35/42
text:
## 14. MISCELLANEOUS  
### 14.1 Entire Agreement  
These Terms & Conditions are the complete agreement between us. They supersede any prior agreements or representations.

name: Chunk 36/42
text:
### 14.2 Severability  
If any term is found invalid, the remaining terms remain in effect.

name: Chunk 37/42
text:
### 14.3 Governing Law  
These terms are governed by **English Law**. Courts of England & Wales have jurisdiction.

name: Chunk 38/42
text:
### 14.4 Contact Information  
**FirstUK Bank Customer Service:**
- Phone: 0800-555-2265 (Mon–Fri 08:00–20:00, Sat 09:00–17:00)
- Email: support@firstuk.bank
- Branch: Visit any FirstUK Bank location  
---

name: Chunk 39/42
text:
## 15. REGULATORY INFORMATION  
**This Account is Protected by:**
- Financial Services Compensation Scheme (FSCS): Up to £85,000 per customer
- Direct Debit Guarantee: Full refund if unauthorized
- FCA Complaints Handling: 8-week investigation  
**Regulator:**
- Financial Conduct Authority (FCA)
- Prudential Regulation Authority (PRA)  
---

name: Chunk 40/42
text:
